title: elymus arenarius var. giganteus
intro: Elymus arenarius var. giganteus, also known as the coastal wheat grass, is a large, perennial grass that is native to the coastal areas of Europe and North Africa. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The health benefits of coastal wheat grass include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of coastal wheat grass are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to treat a variety of health conditions, including digestive issues and skin problems.
